aliases: Pizza with Bell Peppers and Onions

english: Pizza with Bell Peppers and Onions

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for thin-crust meat pizza with minced beef or chicken, bell peppers, and onions. FOODON_00002426 (pizza food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (Pizza) used as closest parent. No specific code exists for Indian-style thin-crust meat pizza with minced beef or chicken topping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Pizza food) used as closest parent. No specific code for this fusion variant.

preparation_method: topped with bell peppers and onions

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole thin-crust Meatzza pizza, approximately 300 g, as typically served at a Singapore restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, meat topping, thin crust — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Meatzza or Indian-style thin-crust meat pizza in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for thin-crust meat pizza (FDC ID 170919 — Pizza with meat, thin crust) and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat and vegetables. Macros adjusted to reflect minced beef or chicken, bell peppers, and onions as primary toppings. Values represent a reasonable composite estimate per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza and mixed meat-topped flatbreads — University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for Meatzza or Indian-style thin-crust meat pizza. Analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for thin-crust pizza with meat toppings, which report GI values in the range of 36–50 (mean approximately 45). Thin crust pizza with protein and fat toppings consistently falls in the low GI range due to fat and protein slowing gastric emptying. GI = 45 selected as a conservative central estimate. GL per serving calculated as (45 × 26.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 300 g serving) / 100 = 35.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (560 mg per 100 g; approximately 1680 mg per whole pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Meatzza consumption due to its high sodium content from processed cheese, tomato sauce, and seasoned minced meat; a single serving may provide 70–84% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load with low GI (45); fat and protein content attenuates postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Meatzza has a low glycemic index of approximately 45, but the glycemic load per whole serving is substantial at approximately 35.6 g; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion and monitor postprandial blood glucose, as the high fat content may also delay but prolong glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (3.9 g per 100 g) and sodium (560 mg per 100 g) from cheese and processed meat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Meatzza contains moderate saturated fat from cheese and minced meat, and high sodium from sauce and seasoning; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to half a pizza and choose the chicken variant over beef to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Meatzza is a thin-crust pizza served at Indian-influenced restaurants in Singapore, notably at #Foodcoholic on Circular Road. It is topped with minced beef or minced chicken (customer's choice), bell peppers, and onions, and is baked on a thin wheat-flour crust with tomato sauce and melted cheese. The name 'Meatzza' is a portmanteau of 'meat' and 'pizza', reflecting its meat-forward topping profile. The dish is classified as a fusion food combining Western pizza preparation with South Asian spice influences typical of Singapore's Indian restaurant scene. Nutritionally, Meatzza provides approximately 242 kcal per 100 g, with 12.5 g protein, 9.8 g fat, and 26.5 g carbohydrate. The thin crust keeps the carbohydrate density moderate, and the protein and fat from the meat and cheese topping contribute to a low glycemic index of approximately 45. A whole pizza serving of approximately 300 g delivers around 726 kcal and 1680 mg sodium, making sodium the primary clinical concern. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. It is likely halal when prepared with certified halal minced meat. Clinically, the high sodium content is the primary concern for hypertension patients, while the moderate glycemic load and saturated fat content require portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: Is Meatzza a thin crust pizza?
Where can I get Meatzza in Singapore?
What is the GI of meat pizza?
Can I eat Meatzza if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Meatzza or this specific Indian-style thin-crust meat pizza was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for thin-crust meat pizza (FDC ID 170919 and SR Legacy analogues). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for thin-crust meat pizza. Confidence is medium because the adaptation is defensible and well-documented but not an exact source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for thin-crust meat pizza. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry used. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, folate, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and water are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite data for thin-crust meat pizza and are reasonable approximations. The dish may be prepared with either minced beef or minced chicken depending on the restaurant's offering; nutrient values represent a composite estimate. Halal status is assumed based on the Indian Muslim restaurant context but is not independently verified. The cuisine tag 'Indian' in the input is retained as the restaurant is Indian-influenced; the dish itself is a fusion product. GI category is 'low' (GI = 45, which is ≤ 55 per Veritas threshold rules).

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names required (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Added all four. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list.

Iteration 2: Verified GI category threshold (GI=45 ≤ 55 = low, correct). Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified zero-macro justification not needed (no core macros are zero). Verified ontology match_type values are all from allowed set. Confirmed score 8/8 and all hard gates pass.
